title: Merck — Vaccines — Knowledge Doc
html:
Market Context: Merck Vaccines Portfolio

Products: GARDASIL 9 (HPV), VAXELIS (pediatric 6-in-1), PNEUMOVAX (pneumococcal), V940 (investigational mRNA cancer vaccine) Manufacturer: Merck & Co. Document scope: Portfolio-level competitive/clinical landscape Last updated: April 2026

1. Disease Landscape

1.1 HPV and Cervical Cancer Prevention

Epidemiology:

  • HPV infects ~80% of sexually active individuals at some point; most infections clear spontaneously
  • ~36,000 HPV-attributable cancers diagnosed annually in the US (cervical, oropharyngeal, anal, vulvar, vaginal, penile)
  • ~4,000 US deaths from cervical cancer annually; >340,000 deaths globally
  • HPV types 16 and 18 cause ~70% of cervical cancers; GARDASIL 9 covers 9 types responsible for ~90%+

Vaccination Rates:

  • US adolescent HPV vaccination (≥1 dose): ~76% of 13-17 year-olds (CDC 2024)
  • Completion of full series: ~62% — the gap between initiation and completion is a persistent challenge
  • Adult catch-up (27-45): <10% uptake — largely untapped market

1.2 Pediatric Immunization

  • US childhood immunization schedule includes 14 vaccines by age 2 — high injection burden per visit
  • Combination vaccines (like VAXELIS 6-in-1) reduce injection count and improve schedule adherence
  • Vaccine hesitancy growing: ~5% of kindergartners exempted from vaccination requirements (up from ~2% pre-pandemic)

1.3 Pneumococcal Disease

  • ~150,000 hospitalizations annually from pneumococcal infections in the US (primarily elderly)
  • PNEUMOVAX 23 covers 23 serotypes but has declining relevance vs. conjugate vaccines (Prevnar)
  • Market shifting toward conjugate vaccines with broader serotype coverage

2. Product Profiles

2.1 GARDASIL 9 (9-valent HPV Vaccine) — Merck

Mechanism: Recombinant vaccine containing virus-like particles (VLPs) from 9 HPV types (6, 11, 16, 18, 31, 33, 45, 52, 58). Induces neutralizing antibodies preventing HPV infection.

Approved Indications:

  • Prevention of cervical, vulvar, vaginal, and anal cancers caused by HPV types 16, 18, 31, 33, 45, 52, 58
  • Prevention of genital warts caused by HPV types 6 and 11
  • Ages 9-45 years

Market Performance:

  • FY2024: ~$8.5B global sales (peak revenue year)
  • FY2025: $5.2B (-39%) — driven by China collapse
  • China 2025: $193M (down from $3.5B in 2024)
  • 2026 guidance: $0 China revenue assumed

Key Dynamics:

  • China crisis: shipments paused Feb 2025 due to Zhifei distributor inventory buildup; domestic 9-valent competitor launched May 2025
  • CDC single-dose recommendation shifting from 2-3 doses to 1 — reduces per-patient revenue by 50-67%
  • US franchise (~$2-3B annually) is stable but faces volume headwinds
  • Cervical cancer elimination campaigns globally still drive demand in developing markets

2.2 VAXELIS — Merck/Sanofi (joint venture)

Mechanism: Hexavalent combination vaccine (DTaP-IPV-Hep B-Hib) — 6 vaccines in one injection.

Approved Indication: Primary and booster immunization of infants and toddlers (6 weeks through 4 years)

Market Position:

  • Reduces injection burden (one shot replaces three separate injections)
  • ZoomRx has tracked via 4 waves of PET studies (2021-2024) — sustained commercial investment
  • Growing adoption as pediatricians prefer fewer injections per visit
  • Competes with individual component vaccines and Sanofi's Hexaxim internationally

2.3 PNEUMOVAX 23 — Merck

Mechanism: 23-valent pneumococcal polysaccharide vaccine.

Market Position:

  • Mature, declining product
  • Being displaced by conjugate vaccines (Pfizer Prevnar 20, Merck Vaxneuvance)
  • Not a growth driver; maintained in portfolio for completeness

2.4 V940 (mRNA-4157/intismeran autogene) — Merck + Moderna

Mechanism: Individualized neoantigen therapy. Patient's tumor is sequenced to identify up to 34 unique neoantigens; a personalized mRNA vaccine is manufactured encoding those neoantigens. Administered with Keytruda to amplify the immune response.

Clinical Data:

  • KEYNOTE-942 (Phase 2b): 5-year data: 49% reduction in recurrence/death in resected Stage III/IV melanoma + Keytruda vs. Keytruda alone
  • INTerpath-001 (Phase 3, melanoma): Fully enrolled; readout expected 2027
  • INTerpath-002 (Phase 3, NSCLC): Enrolling
  • INTerpath-009 (Phase 3, NSCLC): Enrolling; targets patients without pCR after neoadjuvant Keytruda + chemo

Market Potential:

  • First-in-class individualized cancer vaccine — no commercial precedent
  • Estimated $5-10B+ peak potential if approved across multiple solid tumors
  • Manufacturing: requires patient-specific production (~6-8 weeks per patient)
  • Pricing: likely $100K+ per patient — payer willingness undefined
  • Requires Keytruda co-administration — tied to Keytruda's LOE timeline

3. Competitive Dynamics

3.1 HPV Vaccine Competition

Agent Company Type Key Differentiator
GARDASIL 9 Merck 9-valent recombinant Gold standard; broadest coverage
Cervarix GSK Bivalent (types 16, 18) Minimal US presence; used in global programs
Chinese 9-valent Domestic manufacturers 9-valent Lower price; launched May 2025; China-only
Next-gen HPV vaccines Multiple Various Broader coverage, thermostable, single-dose by design

3.2 Pediatric Vaccine Competition

Agent Company Type Key Differentiator
VAXELIS Merck/Sanofi Hexavalent (6-in-1) Fewest injections per visit
Individual components Multiple Separate vaccines Long track record; familiar to practices
Hexaxim Sanofi Hexavalent International competitor to VAXELIS

3.3 Cancer Vaccine Competition

Agent Company Mechanism Stage
V940 Merck/Moderna Individualized mRNA neoantigen Phase 3 (3 trials)
Autogene cevumeran BioNTech/Roche Individualized mRNA neoantigen Phase 2 (pancreatic)
TG4050 Transgene Personalized viral vector Phase 2 (HNSCC, ovarian)

4. Market Context

4.1 Market Size

  • Global HPV vaccine market: ~$8-10B (2024 peak); declining with GARDASIL contraction
  • US pediatric vaccine market: ~$7-9B annually; growing modestly
  • Cancer vaccine market (future): Undefined; V940 could create a $10B+ segment if approved

4.2 Key Market Dynamics

  1. GARDASIL is a franchise in structural transition: China gone, US volume pressured by single-dose. Stable but no longer a growth engine.
  2. V940 is the strategic moonshot: If Phase 3 succeeds, individualized cancer vaccines could become Merck's largest vaccine franchise by 2030.
  3. Vaccine hesitancy is a secular headwind: ~5% kindergarten exemption rates (up from ~2%) create volume risk across the portfolio.
  4. mRNA manufacturing is the V940 bottleneck: Individualized production at commercial scale has never been attempted. Moderna partnership mitigates but doesn't eliminate this risk.
  5. Keytruda LOE linkage: V940 requires Keytruda co-administration. Keytruda's 2028-2029 LOE timeline creates strategic urgency — V940 may need approval before Keytruda loses exclusivity to maximize the combination therapy window.

5. Pipeline

Program Indication Phase Status
V940 INTerpath-001 Resected melanoma Phase 3 Fully enrolled; readout ~2027
V940 INTerpath-002 NSCLC (adjuvant) Phase 3 Enrolling
V940 INTerpath-009 NSCLC (non-pCR after neoadjuvant) Phase 3 Enrolling (first patients Canada)
GARDASIL next-gen Broader HPV coverage Research Early; thermostable formulations explored
Vaxneuvance Pneumococcal (15-valent) Approved Competing with Pfizer Prevnar 20

6. Key Themes for Tracking

Theme Tracking Question Priority
GARDASIL US franchise How does single-dose CDC guidance affect physician behavior and revenue per patient? HIGH
V940 oncologist awareness What do oncologists know about individualized cancer vaccines? Intent to adopt? HIGH
VAXELIS adoption Is pediatric combination vaccine preference growing? MEDIUM
Vaccine hesitancy impact Is hesitancy affecting Merck's overall vaccine revenue trajectory? MEDIUM
V940 manufacturing perception Do oncologists view personalized manufacturing as a barrier or differentiator? LOW (for now)

7. Claims Requiring Verification

High Confidence

  • GARDASIL FY2025 revenue $5.2B (-39%)
  • China GARDASIL 2025: $193M (from $3.5B in 2024)
  • V940 5-year KEYNOTE-942: 49% reduction in recurrence/death
  • INTerpath-001 fully enrolled
  • GARDASIL 9 covers types 6, 11, 16, 18, 31, 33, 45, 52, 58

Moderate Confidence

  • 2026 guidance assumes $0 China revenue — verify against Merck Q1 2026 earnings (pending)
  • Chinese domestic 9-valent launched May 2025 — verify specific manufacturer and pricing
  • CDC single-dose recommendation timing — formal guidance change may be pending
  • VAXELIS growth trajectory — no public revenue breakout (JV with Sanofi)

Lower Confidence

  • V940 $5-10B+ peak potential — analyst estimates, highly speculative
  • V940 manufacturing cost ~$100K+ per patient — no pricing disclosed
  • Vaccine hesitancy ~5% kindergarten exemption — verify against latest CDC data
  • V940 NDA timing (2027-2028) — depends on Phase 3 readouts

This document serves as the portfolio-level competitive/clinical foundation for all tracking studies in the Merck Vaccines space (GARDASIL PET, VAXELIS PET, V940 HCP-Pt, Vaccines Digital Experience). Not wave-versioned — one file per therapeutic area.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
